## AI Assistant Footer Policy

**PR bodies and commit messages MUST NOT include AI co-author footers.**

This includes (non-exhaustive list — anything semantically equivalent is
also banned):

- `Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>`
- `Co-Authored-By: Claude Code <...>`
- `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus <...>` (any model variant or context size)
- `Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)`
- `Generated with Claude` / `Generated by Claude`
- Any other "Generated with ..." or "Co-Authored-By: <AI model>" line that
  encodes the use of an AI assistant.

**Why.** This is a public repository. Including agent provenance in commit
metadata and PR bodies (a) leaks internal tooling choices into the public
git history forever, (b) ties human contributions to a specific
AI-assistant product or version that may not age well, and (c) can break
attribution / contribution tooling that does not expect non-human authors.

If you used an AI assistant to help draft a change, that is fine — but
credit the human author (you) in the commit and PR. The use of AI tooling
is workflow detail, not part of the change's public attribution.

This policy applies to all contributors, including the project owner.
Pre-merge review will reject PRs that contain banned footers; squash-merge
commit messages should be edited to remove them as well.

- [ ] I have removed any AI co-author footers from this PR body and from
      every commit message that will land via squash/rebase.
